title: entitlements — HTTP middleware
description: Owns `entitlement.enforce:<key>` route-parameterised middleware. Consumer modules attach it to routes that trigger metered features so the check runs BEFORE the controller body — saves work when quota exceeded.

middleware.description: Route middleware: `entitlement.enforce:webhook.subscriptions.count`. Runs Enforcer::enforce($key) on the resolved tenant. Throws QuotaExceeded (HTTP 402) with upgrade path in body when cap reached. Runs AFTER auth:sanctum + tenant.resolve so both are already bound.

notes: Middleware-based enforcement is the coarse-grained option. Attribute-based enforcement (#[ConsumesEntitlement] on service methods) is the fine-grained option. Use whichever fits.
Middleware never consumes — only enforces. Consumption happens inside the controller/service after the operation succeeds.
